data.title: BDACS and Woori Bank introduce South Korea's inaugural won-backed stablecoin on Avalanche, adding to Asia's competitive stablecoin market.
data.description: In a significant development, South Korea has officially entered the competitive stablecoin market in Asia by launching its inaugural fully regulated stablecoin backed by the national currency, the won. With this move, South Korea now adds to the growing number of countries in the region entering the stablecoin race. This development underscores the increasing popularity and acceptance of stablecoins in the market, with the country joining the competition with its own unique offering. This announcement comes amid escalating interest in stablecoins, with its market showing significant growth. This move by South Korea is expected to further energize the stablecoin sector, with industry experts closely monitoring the impact of this new player in the market.
Swiss watchmakers fear for the future of their multi-billion dollar industry as Trump hits them with a 35% tariff.

data.title: Kimco Realty (NYSE: KIM) to announce Q3 2025 earnings on Oct 30. Join conference call for insights.
data.description: In Jericho, New York, Kimco Realty (NYSE: KIM) is set to disclose its third-quarter earnings on October 30, 2025, prior to the market opening. The invitation is open to all interested in joining the quarterly earnings conference call. On this date, pertinent financial details will be shared, and individuals can access the webcast for this information.

data.title: TELUS Digital files Circular for Special Meeting on Oct 27, 2025 with TELUS Corporation, ticker symbols TIXT, T, TU.
data.description: TELUS International (Cda) Inc. has disclosed that it submitted its management information circular for the upcoming special meeting of shareholders scheduled to be held online at 9:00 a.m. (Vancouver time) on October 27, 2025. This meeting will address the proposed arrangement with TELUS Corporation, with ticker symbols TSX: T and NYSE: TU. Shareholders will convene virtually to vote on the "Arrangement."

data.title: CZ issues warning as 'SEAL' team uncovers 60 phony IT staff tied to North Korea.譌C-60 impersonators exposed.
data.description: Changpeng Zhao issued a caution about North Korean hackers following an investigation by the white hat SEAL team, which revealed the profiles and aliases of 60 impostors.

data.title: DuPont outlines strategy and financial targets, introduces new portfolio post electronics spin-off. Estimated 3Q 2025: $2.98B in net sales, $805M operating EBITDA, $1.06 adjusted EPS; FY 2025: $6.86B net sales, $1.57B operating EBITDA. Medium-term targets: 3-4% organic sales CAGR, 150-200 bps EBITDA margin improvement, 8-10% adjusted EPS CAGR. Discloses plans for discontinuing Aramids and Qnity electronics businesses. (Kenneth Kwok / Shutterstock.com)
data.description: DuPont presented its new portfolio strategy during its 2025 Investor Day, following the intended spin-off of its electronics business, Qnity Electronics, Inc. The company emphasized a simplified, agile, and high-performing model focused on operational and commercial excellence to drive growth and free cash flow. It provided medium-term financial targets, including 3-4% organic sales growth CAGR, 150-200 basis points improvement in operating EBITDA margin, 8-10% adjusted EPS growth CAGR, and >90% free cash flow conversion by 2028. DuPont announced the recast of its 2025 financial guidance to reflect the divestiture of its Aramids business and Qnity Electronics. For Q3 2025, estimated net sales are $2.980 billion, with operating EBITDA of $805 million and adjusted EPS of $1.06 per share. For FY 2025, estimated net sales are $6.865 billion, and operating EBITDA is $1.575 billion. The company plans to finalize its adjusted EPS guidance during its third quarter 2025 earnings call. DuPont's transformed strategy aims at unlocking significant shareholder value by balancing targeted investments and cash returns, with a strong emphasis on innovation, operational excellence, and continuous improvement. CEO Lori Koch expressed confidence in the company's ability to deliver measurable results and drive profitable growth.

data.title: Commercial Metals Company (CMC) to buy Concrete Pipe & Precast, LLC for $675 million, expanding early-stage construction solutions.
data.description: Commercial Metals Company (CMC) has announced the acquisition of Concrete Pipe & Precast, LLC (CP&P) for $675 million. This move aims to strengthen CMC's commercial portfolio in early-stage construction solutions and deepen its exposure to the construction industry, where CP&P is a significant player with strong margins. The purchase price represents a 9.5x multiple of CP&P's forecasted 2025 EBITDA. The transaction is expected to be immediately accretive to earnings per share and free cash flow per share. By year three, synergies are anticipated to range from $5 million to $10 million. The acquisition is projected to enhance CMC's financial profile, providing a scalable growth platform in an attractive industry with positive long-term trends. This strategic move broadens CMC's construction solutions portfolio, increases its exposure to structural demand tailwinds, and establishes a presence in the precast concrete industry, which generates approximately $30 billion in revenue annually. This entry into a new sector is expected to reinforce CMC's core business and improve its financial standing by bringing in higher, more stable margin earnings.

data.title: JBT Marel released 2024 Sustainability Report, focusing on positive environmental and social impact. CEO Brian Deck emphasizes company's responsibility.
data.description: In Chicago, JBT Marel Corporation, a prominent player in food and beverage technology solutions, has unveiled its 2024 Sustainability Report titled "Shaping the Future of Food Together." The report, now available to the public, sheds light on the company's efforts to generate significant value for clients, staff, communities, and the broader food industry. Brian Deck, the CEO, emphasized the importance of sustainability, stating, "It's not just a target; it's our duty." Traded on NYSE and Nasdaq Iceland as JBTM, the corporation demonstrates its commitment to shaping a more sustainable future through this report.

data.title: European DataWarehouse Ltd. in UK appoints Nigel Batley as Executive Director, succeeding Markus Schaber. Batley has 40 years of experience in financial services industry, including over 30 years at HSBC. Schaber served as Executive Director since 2020.
data.description: European DataWarehouse Ltd. (EDW Ltd.), the UK branch of European DataWarehouse GmbH, has appointed Nigel Batley as the new Executive Director. Batley takes over from Markus Schaber, who held the position since 2020 and is now retiring. Batley, with nearly four decades of experience in the financial services sector, notably in asset-backed finance, previously worked at HSBC for over 30 years in various senior roles. This transition brings a change in leadership to the organization with significant industry knowledge and expertise.
Investor holds 8.3% in Empiric Student Property Plc.

data.title: Radian to purchase Inigo, a profitable Lloyd's insurer, for $1.7B, transforming Radian into a global specialty insurer.
data.description: In Wayne, Pennsylvania, Radian Group Inc. (NYSE: RDN) has revealed its plan to purchase Inigo Limited, a prosperous Lloyd’s specialty insurer, in a deal worth $1.7 billion, primarily in cash. The acquisition, financed through Radian’s liquid assets and additional capital from its subsidiaries, signifies a significant shift in the company's focus from being a prominent U.S. mortgage insurer to a more diversified entity.

data.title: Maelstrom Fund, Animoca Brands join $6.9M investment in Bio Protocol to combine AI, biotech, crypto for scientific progress.
data.description: Bio Protocol has successfully raised an impressive $6.9 million in funding from Maelstrom Fund, Animoca Brands, as well as other investors. This capital infusion will be utilized to propel scientific advancements through the innovative integration of artificial intelligence and blockchain technology.

data.title: Immuto Scientific secures $8M in seed financing and partners with Daiichi Sankyo for research on therapeutic targets.
data.description: A partnership has been forged between Immuto Scientific, a biotech company based in Madison, Wis., and Daiichi Sankyo, a pharmaceutical company in Cambridge, Mass. The collaboration includes a significant financing deal and aims to identify a novel set of therapeutic targets utilizing Structural Surfaceomics. This innovative approach combines both financial investment and technical expertise to uncover potential breakthroughs in the field.
